steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says store context may 'moderate' the spacing effect — in research, moderate means to reduce or weaken. The original effect: products spaced far apart are perceived as more valuable. If store context moderates this, the spacing effect should be weaker at an inexpensive store (Forever 21) than at an upscale store (Tiffany & Co.).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: shows a uniform spacing effect across both stores — no moderation at all. ✗
  • Choice B: shows the spacing effect stronger at the inexpensive store — the opposite of moderation. ✗
  • Choice C: shows a substantial spacing effect at Tiffany & Co. but only a slight one at Forever 21 — the effect is weakened at the inexpensive store, directly supporting moderation. ✓
  • Choice D: reverses the spacing relationship entirely — products close together are judged more valuable, contradicting the stated effect. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
